mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates. This triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as market participants reprice growth expectations downward.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates above neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns remains below 0.15 in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% annualized rate while simultaneously housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1.2 million units annualized. This joint contraction in consumer and housing demand signals weakening aggregate demand, producing negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth < 2% annualized AND HOUST < 1.2M units annualized
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction across consumer and housing sectors
failure_condition: Joint occurrence of both conditions produces positive returns in > 50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while the index remains below 104.0, indicating supply-side weakness and potential stagflationary pressures. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice inflation-adjusted earnings.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth accelerates above 250K new jobs per month while simultaneously the unemployment rate remains below 4.0%. This dual strength in labor market conditions signals robust consumer income and spending power, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: tight labor market with strong employment growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances meeting both conditions, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates to below 3.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains above 5.0%, signaling that monetary policy is restrictive relative to inflation trends. This real rate elevation produces positive equity returns over 60 trading days as inflation expectations normalize and policy easing becomes anticipated.
regime_hint: disinflation phase with restrictive monetary policy stance
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between inflation deceleration and high rates is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains positive and above 2.0% annualized while housing starts (HOUST) exceed 1.3 million units annualized, indicating sustained economic expansion with healthy residential investment. This mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings growth expectations remain elevated.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of periods when both conditions hold, or correlation between GDPC1 growth and forward returns is below 0.10 in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month while simultaneously CPIAUCSL inflation remains below 3.5% year-over-year. This combination signals healthy supply-side expansion without inflationary pressure, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as profit margins expand.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth > 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL YoY < 3.5%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between supply growth and low inflation is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
